name: Santorcaz and Santa Casilda Loop
name_original: Subida a San Torcaz — circular desde Santuario de Doña Casilda
description: This difficult 7.2-mile (11.7 km) jogging route ascends to San Torcaz from Santuario de Doña Casilda, gaining 1097 feet (334 metres).
region_intro: Jogging routes around Salinillas De Bureba traverse a serene natural environment characterized by gentle hills, expansive open fields, and traditional paths connecting neighboring villages. The area features rural tracks and agricultural fields, offering relatively flat or gently undulating terrain suitable for running. Notable natural features include the Cascadas de Salinillas de Bureba, with paths winding through oak groves, and the natural setting surrounding the Santuario de Santa Casilda. Best jogging routes around Salinillas De Bureba The most popular jogging route is Santorcaz and Santa Casilda Loop, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes a significant elevation gain, ascending to San Torcaz from the Santuario de Santa Casilda. Another top favourite among local runners is Murals of Reinoso loop from Reinoso de Bureba, a difficult 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and takes approximately 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. Local runners also love the Running loop from Monasterio de Rodilla, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 0 minutes. Jogging around Salinillas De Bureba is defined by gentle hills, open fields, and paths leading to natural features like waterfalls and oak groves. The network offers options for both leisurely runs and more challenging workouts across diverse terrain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 5 runners have used komoot to explore Salinillas De Bureba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Salinillas De Bureba?
A: Komoot currently features &lt;strong&gt;3 diverse running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Salinillas De Bureba. These trails offer a range of experiences, from moderate to difficult, allowing you to explore the serene natural environment of the La Bureba region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Salinillas De Bureba?
A: The jogging trails in Salinillas De Bureba traverse a varied landscape characterized by &lt;em&gt;gentle hills, expansive open fields, and traditional rural paths&lt;/em&gt;. You'll find agricultural tracks that are relatively flat or gently undulating, as well as paths winding through oak groves, especially near natural features like waterfalls. The broader La Bureba region also offers diverse terrain, including forests and river canyons.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Salinillas De Bureba?
A: While Salinillas De Bureba offers some challenging routes, there are options suitable for those looking for a more moderate run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Briviesca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.6 km (4.7 miles) path with minimal elevation gain, making it a good choice for a less strenuous outing.

Q: Can I find routes that feature waterfalls in Salinillas De Bureba?
A: Yes, the region is home to the beautiful &lt;strong&gt;Cascadas de Salinillas de Bureba&lt;/strong&gt;, including Peña Borco and "La Herradura" (Las Calderas). Paths to these waterfalls begin at the end of the village and ascend through an oak grove, offering a picturesque and stimulating experience, especially during wetter seasons.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see along the running paths?
A: Absolutely! The area around Salinillas De Bureba is rich in history. You can explore the natural setting surrounding the &lt;strong&gt;Santuario de Santa Casilda&lt;/strong&gt;, which combines historical interest with natural beauty. Other nearby highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Doña Casilda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lences de Bureba Village and Historic Sites&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is a challenging running route for experienced runners in the area?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051296464/santorcaz-and-santa-casilda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santorcaz and Santa Casilda Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.7 km (7.2 miles) trail. This route involves a significant elevation gain of 334 meters (1097 feet) as it ascends to San Torcaz from the Santuario de Santa Casilda, offering a rewarding workout and stunning views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Monasterio de Rodilla&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.3 km (5.1 miles) circular trail that takes you through the local countryside, perfect for a continuous run.

Q: What are some other notable points of interest near the running trails?
A: Beyond the main routes, you can discover other interesting spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4496454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Negro&lt;/a&gt; is a natural monument worth exploring, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4427452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Village Fountain in Monasterio de Rodilla&lt;/a&gt; offers a charming local highlight.

Q: Is there a route that showcases local art or unique features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murals of Reinoso loop from Reinoso de Bureba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.2 km (8.8 miles) path that offers varied terrain and likely passes by the unique murals of Reinoso, providing a cultural element to your run.

Q: What makes Salinillas De Bureba a good destination for outdoor activities like jogging?
A: Salinillas De Bureba is an excellent destination for outdoor activities due to its &lt;em&gt;serene natural environment, varied landscapes, and established trails&lt;/em&gt;. The tranquil setting, characterized by gentle hills, expansive open fields, and traditional paths, provides an ideal backdrop for an active escape amidst nature, catering to both leisurely runs and more challenging workouts.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Salinillas De Bureba?
A: The region's climate generally allows for pleasant jogging during many months. While the Cascadas de Salinillas de Bureba are most spectacular during rainy or snowy seasons, the trails themselves are enjoyable year-round. Spring and autumn often offer mild temperatures ideal for running, though summer mornings and evenings can also be very pleasant.
tips: This Sanctuary is located in a magnificent location, it is worth the site, the views, and the cave of Doña Casilda (in the back of the Monastery).
Accessible from the south (Doña Casilda) or from the north (Piérnigas). The views are very nice.

Due to popular beliefs, curative properties are attributed to the Pozo Negro de Santa Casilda. The area recovered by the Friends of Santa Casilda is the perfect place to rest for a while before going up to the Sanctuary.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The Ascent to San Torcaz — circular from Santuario de Doña Casilda is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete as a jogging route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' grading, this route is likely more suitable for experienced joggers or those with a good level of fitness. The elevation gain of over 330 meters also contributes to its challenging nature.

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to San Torcaz circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is described as circular from the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda. While specific parking details for this exact trail are not available, it's common for religious sites like sanctuaries to have some form of parking nearby. We recommend checking local information for the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda closer to your visit.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Ascent to San Torcaz route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda, the San Torcaz summit, and the natural monument known as Pozo Negro. These highlights suggest a mix of cultural and natural points of interest.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to San Torcaz — circular from Santuario de Doña Casilda dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular trail are not detailed. Generally, trails in natural areas often permit dogs on a leash, but it's always best to verify local rules, especially around religious sites like the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda or protected natural monuments like Pozo Negro, before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required for jogging the Ascent to San Torcaz — circular from Santuario de Doña Casilda route. However, it's always wise to check for any local regulations or fees that might apply to the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda or the Pozo Negro area before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for jogging?
faqs.answer: Without specific local climate data for this exact trail, general advice for jogging in mountainous or hilly regions applies. Spring and autumn often offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder weather or snow, especially at higher elevations like San Torcaz.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: Specific details about the terrain for this exact 'Ascent to San Torcaz — circular from Santuario de Doña Casilda' trail are not available. However, given its 'difficult' grading and significant elevation changes, you can expect varied terrain that could include steep ascents and descents, potentially rocky or uneven paths, and possibly some paved sections near the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the starting point?
faqs.answer: Information specific to this trail is limited. If the Sanctuary of Doña Casilda is located in a populated area, there may be amenities nearby. We recommend researching the immediate vicinity of the sanctuary for local services before your trip.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Rojas (6%) and the Camino de Rojas (4%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this route?
faqs.answer: For a 'difficult' jogging route with over 330 meters of elevation gain, essential items include appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended, especially since specific trail details are scarce.
